name: Medina de Pomar and Ojo Guareña Loop
name_original: De Medina de Pomar a San Pantaléon de Losa — circular por tierra de Las Merindades
description: This moderate 38.2-mile road cycling loop from Medina de Pomar to San Pantaléon de Losa offers scenic views and cultural highlights.
region_intro: Road cycling around Medina De Pomar is characterized by rolling hills and uneven terrain, offering varied routes with both challenging climbs and descents. The region, situated in Las Merindades, features the sharp outline of the Sierra de Tesla and is traversed by the Nela, Trueba, and Salón rivers. Pine forests and scenic views, including the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument, contribute to the diverse landscape for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Medina De Pomar The most popular road cycling route is Ojo Guareña Natural Monument Loop, a 44.5 miles (71.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete, exploring the natural monument's surroundings. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Medina de Pomar and Ojo Guareña Loop, a moderate 38.2 miles (61.4 km) path. This route offers a scenic journey through the local landscape, including views of the Ojo Guareña area. Local road cyclists also love the Church of Vega de Pas – Pozo Guarguero Waterfall loop from Medina de Pomar, a 67.3 miles (108.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards a waterfall, often completed in about 5 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Medina De Pomar is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra de Tesla. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Medina De Pomar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Medina De Pomar?
A: The region around Medina De Pomar is characterized by &lt;strong&gt;rolling hills and uneven terrain&lt;/strong&gt;, offering a diverse experience for road cyclists. You'll encounter both challenging climbs and enjoyable descents. The dramatic backdrop of the Sierra de Tesla provides opportunities for more strenuous rides, while routes along the Nela, Trueba, and Salón rivers often offer flatter, scenic options through pine forests.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Medina De Pomar area?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 25 road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Medina De Pomar documented on komoot, catering to various fitness levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 10 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, there are a few easier routes available. While many routes feature rolling hills, some offer less elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Constables – Villarcayo Town Hall loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option with less significant elevation changes, making it a good choice for those seeking a less strenuous journey.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks and natural features. You can cycle towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6318262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt; area, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats and Old Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purón River Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4004660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Leron viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible along various routes, offering picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, if you're looking to incorporate a waterfall into your ride, consider the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Vega de Pas – Pozo Guarguero Waterfall loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt;. This route leads through varied terrain towards the Pozo Guarguero Waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1871481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3970163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tartalés de los Montes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are highlights in the wider region that could be incorporated into longer rides.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Medina De Pomar?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but the diverse landscapes, including riverside routes and forested areas, can offer some respite. Winter might present colder conditions, especially in higher elevations like the Sierra de Tesla.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40320431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering over 100 km with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Vega de Pas – Pozo Guarguero Waterfall loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; is another demanding option, stretching over 108 km with substantial climbing.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument area?
A: Yes, the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument is a prominent feature in the region and is included in several road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057547215/ojo-guarena-natural-monument-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo Guareña Natural Monument Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 71.6 km trail that explores the monument's surroundings. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058322466/medina-de-pomar-and-ojo-guarena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina de Pomar and Ojo Guareña Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 61.4 km path offering scenic views of the area.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about cycling in Medina De Pomar?
A: Over 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Medina De Pomar's varied terrain. They often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills to river valleys, and the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra de Tesla. The region is appreciated for offering a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels.

Q: Are the road surfaces generally well-maintained for road bikes?
A: Many routes in the Medina De Pomar area feature mostly &lt;strong&gt;well-paved surfaces&lt;/strong&gt;, making them suitable for road cycling. While the region is known for its uneven terrain, the main cycling paths are generally in good condition. Some routes might have short unpaved segments, offering variety for those with appropriate bikes or seeking a mixed-terrain experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through charming villages or historical sites?
A: Yes, cycling routes in Las Merindades often pass through charming villages, allowing for cultural immersion. Medina de Pomar itself boasts a rich historical center with monuments like the Alcázar de los Condestables and the Church of Santa Cruz. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Constables – Villarcayo Town Hall loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that starts near a historical site and explores the local area.
tips: From this point on the road you can see the views of the center of Villabasil and where you can see the Montes de la Peña behind them.

From the bottom of the Peña Colorada is the Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de las Nieves. The road takes you to the foot of the stone where on top of it is the Ermita de San Pantaleón de Losa. Spectacular!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the road cycling route from Medina de Pomar to San Pantaléon de Losa?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is approximately 61.4 kilometers long and generally takes about 2 hours and 55 minutes to complete. Cyclists should have good fitness due to the 770 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly well-paved and suitable for road bikes. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved and difficult to ride, adding an adventurous element and requiring careful navigation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include scenic Views of the village of Villabasil and the culturally significant San Pantaléon de Losa, home to a hermitage recognized as a Bien de Interés Cultural.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Classified as a 'moderate' ride with 770 meters of elevation gain, this route requires good fitness. While the surface is mostly well-paved, the elevation changes and potential unpaved sections mean it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Tierra de Las Merindades region is best enjoyed during seasons with pleasant weather, typically spring and early autumn, to avoid extreme heat or cold. This allows for comfortable cycling through the rolling hills and charming villages.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route in Medina de Pomar?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Medina de Pomar is the starting point, and you should look for public parking options within the town. It's advisable to check local regulations for parking availability for cyclists.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths, including the Camino de Las Merindades, Ruta de los Sentidos, Maratón Alpina Medinesa 42 KM, and Caminos de Medina, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially since it's near Monumento Natural de Ojo Guareña?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this road route itself. While it traverses the Tierra de Las Merindades, which is home to the Monumento Natural de Ojo Guareña, the cycling route does not directly enter areas of the natural monument that would require permits or fees for passage.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically considered a 'dog-friendly' activity in the same way a hiking trail might be. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always adhere to local leash laws and waste disposal regulations in villages.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation available in Medina de Pomar or near San Pantaléon de Losa?
faqs.answer: Medina de Pomar, as the starting point, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists. San Pantaléon de Losa is a smaller destination, so while it has cultural significance, amenities there might be limited compared to Medina de Pomar.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect to cycle through the scenic Tierra de Las Merindades, characterized by charming villages and rolling hills. The region is known for its natural beauty and cultural heritage, offering picturesque views of rural life in Castilla y León.

waypoints.name: Views of the village of Villabasil
